content:
# Leviticus Chapter 17 — The EMET Bible (Literal)

**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 68r–68v | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/LEV_017_page_001.jpg`, `LEV_017_page_002.jpg` | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 103–104 | `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/LEV_017_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `LEV_017_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

*[Note on structure: Leviticus 17 is a transitional chapter of extraordinary theological density. It stands between the cultic regulations of chapters 1–16 (the "Priestly core" ending with the Day of Atonements) and the holiness legislation of chapters 18–27 (the "Holiness Code"). Its position is not accidental: having established in chapter 16 the supreme mechanism of atonement — the annual blood rite at the kapporet — chapter 17 now legislates the theology of blood that undergirds that entire system. The chapter divides into three sections: (1) vv.1–9: the requirement that all slaughter of animals — whether sacrificial or non-sacrificial — be brought to the entrance of the tent of meeting; unauthorized slaughter anywhere else constitutes a capital offense, equivalent to murder (dam yechashev, "blood shall be imputed"); (2) vv.10–14: the absolute prohibition of consuming blood, grounded in the theological statement that is the chapter's theological center: ki nefesh ha-basar ba-dam hi, "for the life/soul of the flesh is in the blood" — a statement of such consequence that it generates the altar-logic of the entire sacrificial system, and its prohibition is repeated three times (vv.10, 12, 14); (3) vv.15–16: the case of naturally dead or torn animals — one who eats such becomes impure until evening after washing. Chapter 17 thus moves from pure ritual law (where to slaughter) to cultic theology (blood as the bearer of nefesh) to a transitional case that leads toward the purity concerns of chapters 18–27. The two key concepts — nefesh in the blood and dam imputed like a murder — are among the most theologically weighty in the Hebrew Bible, foundational to later discussions of atonement in both Rabbinic and New Testament theology.]*

---

**1** And the LORD [YHWH] spoke [vayedabber] to Moshe [el-Moshe] [el-Moshe — "to Moshe"; address to Moshe alone as in 16:1; LXX: Καὶ ἐλάλησεν κύριος πρὸς Μωυσῆν (Kai elalēsen kyrios pros Mōysēn, "And the LORD spoke to Moshe")], saying [lemor] [lemor — "saying"; the standard formula introducing divine speech; LXX: λέγων (legōn, "saying")]:

**2** "Speak [dabber] to Aharon [el-Aharon] [el-Aharon — "to Aharon"; LXX: Λάλησον Ααρων (Lalēson Aarōn, "Speak to Aharon")] and to his sons [ve-el-banav] [ve-el-banav — "and to his sons"; the priestly household, not the congregation; LXX: καὶ τοῖς υἱοῖς αὐτοῦ (kai tois huiois autou, "and to his sons")] and to all the sons of Yisra'el [ve-el-kol benei Yisra'el] [ve-el-kol benei Yisra'el — "and to all the sons of Yisra'el"; this is a remarkably inclusive address — priest and layperson alike; the law applies to the entire community; LXX: καὶ πρὸς πάντας υἱοὺς Ισραηλ (kai pros pantas huious Israēl, "and to all the sons of Yisra'el")] and say to them [ve-amarta alehem] [ve-amarta alehem — "and you shall say to them"; the two-verb formula dabber...ve-amarta signals both proclamation and direct address; LXX: καὶ ἐρεῖς πρὸς αὐτούς (kai ereis pros autous, "and you shall say to them")]: 'This is the thing [zeh ha-davar] [zeh ha-davar — "this is the word / the thing"; the davar formula introduces the statute as a formal declaration; LXX: Τοῦτό ἐστιν τὸ ῥῆμα (Touto estin to hrēma, "This is the word/matter")] which the LORD has commanded [asher-tzivah YHWH] [asher-tzivah YHWH — "which the LORD commanded"; qal perfect: a completed divine command; LXX: ὃ ἐνετείλατο κύριος (ho eneteilato kyrios, "which the LORD commanded")], saying [lemor]:

**3** Any man from the house of Yisra'el [ish ish mi-beit Yisra'el] [ish ish mi-beit Yisra'el — "a man, a man from the house of Yisra'el"; the doubled ish ish (literally "a man, a man") is a distributive Hebrew idiom meaning "any man whatsoever, every single man"; it casts the widest possible net; LXX: ἄνθρωπος ἄνθρωπος τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ (anthrōpos anthrōpos tōn huiōn Israēl, "a man, a man from the sons of Yisra'el") — the LXX renders "sons of Yisra'el" rather than "house of Yisra'el"] who slaughters [asher yishchat] [asher yishchat — "who slaughters"; qal imperfect of shachat: "to slaughter, to kill an animal (with ritual cutting)"; LXX: ὃς ἐὰν σφάξῃ (hos ean sphaxē, "whoever slaughters")] a bull [shor] [shor — "an ox / a bull / cattle"; the general term for bovine animals; LXX: μόσχον (moschon, "a calf/young bull") — the LXX uses the more specific term] or a lamb [kesev] [kesev — "a sheep / a lamb"; the ovine category; LXX: ἢ πρόβατον (ē probaton, "or a sheep")] or a goat [ve-ez] [ve-ez — "or a goat"; the caprine category; thus: ox, sheep, or goat — the three species of sacrificable domesticated livestock; LXX: ἢ αἶγα (ē aiga, "or a goat")] in the camp [ba-machaneh] [ba-machaneh — "in the camp"; the Israelite camp in the wilderness; the setting presupposed is the wilderness journey with the tabernacle at the center; LXX: ἐν τῇ παρεμβολῇ (en tē parembolē, "in the encampment")] or who slaughters [o asher yishchat] outside the camp [mi-chuts la-machaneh] [mi-chuts la-machaneh — "from outside to the camp / outside the camp"; the preposition mi-chuts ("from outside") with directional preposition la- ("toward"): "outside the camp"; this covers all possible slaughter locations: inside the camp or outside; LXX: ἢ ὃς σφάζει ἔξω τῆς παρεμβολῆς (ē hos sphazei exō tēs parembolēs, "or whoever slaughters outside the encampment")]:

**4** And he has not brought it [ve-el-petach ohel mo'ed lo' hevi'o] [ve-el-petach ohel mo'ed lo' hevi'o — "and to the entrance of the tent of meeting he did not bring it"; the negative lo' (not) + perfect hevi'o: failure to bring the animal to the proper location; petach ohel mo'ed = "the entrance of the tent of meeting" — the appointed place for presentation of all offerings; LXX: καὶ ἐπὶ τὴν θύραν τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου μὴ ἐνέγκῃ αὐτό (kai epi tēn thyran tēs skēnēs tou martyriou mē enegkē auto, "and to the door of the tent of testimony he did not bring it")] to the entrance of the tent of meeting [el-petach ohel mo'ed] to bring it near [le-haqriv] [le-haqriv — "to bring near / to present as an offering"; hiphil infinitive of qarav: "to cause to approach"; the bringing to the tent entrance is equated with presenting an offering; LXX: ὥστε προσενέγκαι (hōste prosenegkai, "so as to offer")] as an offering to the LORD [qorban la-YHWH] [qorban la-YHWH — "a gift/offering to the LORD"; qorban [קָרְבָּן] is the standard term for any offering presented to the LORD (from qarav, "to draw near"); it encompasses burnt offerings, peace offerings, grain offerings, etc.; LXX: δῶρον κυρίῳ (dōron kyriō, "a gift to the LORD"), using δῶρον (dōron, "gift, offering")] before the tabernacle of the LORD [lifnei mishkan YHWH] [lifnei mishkan YHWH — "before the dwelling-place of the LORD"; mishkan [מִשְׁכָּן] = "tabernacle, dwelling-place, residence" (from shakan, "to dwell"); the LORD's permanent residence in the camp; LXX: ἀπέναντι τῆς σκηνῆς κυρίου (apenanti tēs skēnēs kyriou, "before the tabernacle of the LORD"), using σκηνή (skēnē, "tent, tabernacle")] — blood shall be imputed [dam yechashev] [dam yechashev — "blood shall be reckoned / blood shall be imputed"; dam = "blood"; yechashev = niphal imperfect of chashav ("to reckon, to compute, to impute, to account"): "shall be accounted/imputed/reckoned"; the passive niphal makes YHWH the implicit agent of the reckoning; dam yechashev = the slaughter outside the approved place is treated as bloodguilt, equivalent to murder; blood unlawfully spilled — even of an animal — is "accounted" as the blood of a person; LXX: αἷμα λογισθήσεται τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ ἐκείνῳ (haima logisthēsetai tō anthrōpō ekeinō, "blood shall be reckoned to that man"), using λογίζομαι (logizomai, "to reckon, to impute, to account") — the cognate LXX term is logisthēsetai, which Paul uses in Romans 4 of righteousness imputed by faith; the theological weight of chashav/logizomai carries far beyond this verse] to that man [la-ish ha-hu'] [la-ish ha-hu' — "to that man"; dative of accountability: the blood is charged to the account of the one who slaughtered outside the proper place; LXX: τῷ ἀνθρώπῳ ἐκείνῳ (tō anthrōpō ekeinō, "to that man")] — blood [dam] he has shed [shafach] [dam shafach — "blood he has shed"; shafach = "to pour out, to spill, to shed (blood)"; the verb is used of murder (Gen 9:6); to slaughter an animal outside the sanctuary is treated as equivalent to murdering a human being, because the animal's nefesh (life/soul, v.11) belongs to YHWH; LXX: αἷμα ἐξέχεεν (haima execheen, "blood he poured out"), using ἐκχέω (ekcheō, "to pour out, to shed")] — and that man [ve-ha-ish ha-hu'] shall be cut off [ve-nikhrat] [ve-nikhrat — "and shall be cut off"; niphal perfect/imperfect of karat: "to be cut off"; the karet penalty [כָּרֵת] is the most severe punishment in Levitical law — typically meaning exclusion from the covenant community, divine cutting off, and often death (whether human or divine execution); LXX: ἐξολεθρευθήσεται (exolethreuthēsetai, "shall be utterly destroyed"), using ἐξολεθρεύω (exolothreuō, "to destroy utterly, to exterminate")] from the midst of his people [mi-qerev ammo] [mi-qerev ammo — "from the midst of his people"; qerev = "interior, midst, inward parts"; the covenant community from which the offender is excised; LXX: ἐκ τοῦ λαοῦ αὐτοῦ (ek tou laou autou, "from his people")]:

**5** This is in order that [le-ma'an] [le-ma'an — "in order that / to the end that"; a purpose clause; the reason for the regulation is now stated explicitly; LXX: ὅπως (hopōs, "in order that")] the sons of Yisra'el [benei Yisra'el] shall bring [yavi'u] [yavi'u — "shall bring"; hiphil imperfect: "shall cause to come, shall bring"; LXX: προσαγάγωσι (prosagagōsi, "shall bring near")] their sacrifices [et-zivcheihem] [et-zivcheihem — "their sacrifices"; zevach [זֶבַח] (pl. zevachim) = "sacrifice, slaughtered offering"; from zabach ("to sacrifice, to slaughter"); the word encompasses any slaughter for offering; LXX: τὰς θυσίας αὐτῶν (tas thysias autōn, "their sacrifices"), using θυσία (thysia, "sacrifice, offering")] which they sacrifice [asher hem zovechim] [asher hem zovechim — "which they are sacrificing"; a participial clause: "which they offer/keep sacrificing"; present continuous: an ongoing practice is being addressed; LXX: ἃς αὐτοὶ σφάζουσιν (has autoi sphazousin, "which they themselves slaughter")] upon the face of the open field [al-penei ha-sadeh] [al-penei ha-sadeh — "upon the face of the open field / upon the open countryside"; sadeh = "field, open land, countryside, territory"; the "open field" is the unauthorized location where Israelites have apparently been sacrificing outside the camp; the implication is that they were sacrificing to field-spirits or simply apart from the tabernacle; LXX: ἐπὶ τοῦ πεδίου (epi tou pediou, "upon the plain/field"), using πεδίον (pedion, "plain, open field")] — and they shall bring them [ve-hevi'um] [ve-hevi'um — "and shall bring them"; hiphil: "shall bring them"; the object suffix -um ("them") refers to the sacrifices; LXX: καὶ προσοίσουσιν αὐτά (kai prosoisousin auta, "and shall bring them near")] to the LORD [la-YHWH] [la-YHWH — "to the LORD"; LXX: κυρίῳ (kyriō, "to the LORD")] to the entrance of the tent of meeting [el-petach ohel mo'ed] [el-petach ohel mo'ed — as in v.4; the proper place of offering; LXX: πρὸς τὴν θύραν τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου (pros tēn thyran tēs skēnēs tou martyriou, "to the door of the tent of testimony")] to the priest [el-ha-kohen] [el-ha-kohen — "to the priest"; the intermediary; LXX: πρὸς τὸν ἱερέα (pros ton hierea, "to the priest")] and they shall sacrifice them [ve-zavcham] [ve-zavcham — "and shall sacrifice them"; qal perfect / waw-consecutive: "and they shall slaughter them"; zabach = "to sacrifice, to slaughter for offering"; LXX: καὶ θύσουσι θυσίαν (kai thysousin thysian, "and shall sacrifice a sacrifice")] as peace offerings [zivchei shelamim] [zivchei shelamim — "sacrifices of peace offerings"; shelamim [שְׁלָמִים] = "peace offerings, well-being offerings" (from shalom/shalem, "wholeness, peace, completion"); zivchei shelamim is the standard term for the class of offering characterized by shared consumption between the offerer, the priest, and YHWH; LXX: σωτηρίου (sōtēriou, "of deliverance/salvation"), using σωτήριον (sōtērion, "salvation, deliverance") — the LXX renders shelamim as "peace/salvation offering," carrying soteriological overtones in the Greek] to the LORD [la-YHWH]:

**6** And the priest shall dash [ve-zaraq ha-kohen] [ve-zaraq ha-kohen — "and the priest shall dash / shall sprinkle/throw"; zaraq = "to dash, to sprinkle, to throw (liquid)"; specifically the action of throwing blood against the altar base or sides from a bowl, as distinct from hizzah ("to sprinkle finely"); LXX: καὶ προσχεεῖ ὁ ἱερεύς (kai prōscheei ho hiereus, "and the priest shall pour upon"), using προσχέω (proscheomai, "to pour upon, to pour against")] the blood [et-ha-dam] [et-ha-dam — "the blood"; the blood of the peace offering; LXX: τὸ αἷμα (to haima, "the blood")] upon the altar of the LORD [al-mizbeath YHWH] [al-mizbeath YHWH — "upon the altar of the LORD"; the outer altar of burnt offering in the court of the tabernacle; LXX: ἐπὶ τὸ θυσιαστήριον κυρίου (epi to thysiastērion kyriou, "upon the altar of the LORD")] at the entrance of the tent of meeting [petach ohel mo'ed] [petach ohel mo'ed — as in vv.4–5; the altar stands at the entrance; LXX: παρὰ τὴν θύραν τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου (para tēn thyran tēs skēnēs tou martyriou, "beside the door of the tent of testimony")] and he shall burn [ve-hiqtir] [ve-hiqtir — "and shall burn (as incense / on the altar)"; hiphil of qatar: "to cause to go up in smoke, to make smoke, to burn as an offering"; the technical term for burning the fat portions on the altar; LXX: καὶ ἀνοίσει (kai anoisei, "and shall offer up"), using ἀναφέρω (anapherō, "to bring up, to offer up")] the fat [et-ha-chelev] [et-ha-chelev — "the fat"; chelev [חֵלֶב] = "the fat, suet"; specifically the internal body-fat (kidneys, loins, liver, stomach-fat) reserved for YHWH and never eaten; distinct from the regular fat of an animal (called shuman); LXX: τὸ στέαρ (to stear, "the fat"), using στέαρ (stear, "suet, fat of animals")] as a soothing aroma [le-reyach nichoach] [le-reyach nichoach — "for a soothing aroma / for a smell of rest"; reyach = "smell, aroma, scent"; nichoach [נִיחֹחַ] = "resting, soothing, pleasing" (from nuach, "to rest"): together = "a pleasing/soothing aroma"; the formula reyach nichoach la-YHWH ("a pleasing aroma to the LORD") is the standard expression for an accepted sacrifice; the imagery is anthropomorphic: the LORD "inhales" the ascending smoke with pleasure; LXX: εἰς ὀσμὴν εὐωδίας (eis osmēn euōdias, "for a scent of sweet smell"), using ὀσμή (osmē, "smell, odor") and εὐωδία (euōdia, "fragrance, sweet smell")] to the LORD [la-YHWH]:

**7** And they shall no longer [ve-lo'-yizbechu od] [ve-lo'-yizbechu od — "and they shall not sacrifice again / they shall sacrifice no more"; lo' + qal imperfect + od ("again, any more, still"): the absolute cessation of the prohibited practice; LXX: καὶ οὐ θύσουσιν ἔτι (kai ou thysousin eti, "and they shall no longer sacrifice")] their sacrifices [et-zivcheihem] [et-zivcheihem — "their sacrifices"; as in v.5; LXX: τὰς θυσίας αὐτῶν (tas thysias autōn, "their sacrifices")] to the goat-demons [la-se'irim] [la-se'irim — "to the goat-demons / to the hairy ones / to the satyrs"; se'irim [שְׂעִירִים] = plural of sa'ir ("goat, hairy one, he-goat"); in cultic contexts sa'ir / se'irim denotes demonic beings, spirits inhabiting desolate places, conceived in goat-like form; Isa 13:21 and 34:14 use se'irim alongside lilith and jackals in the depiction of desolate ruins; the word derives from sa'ar ("hair") — these are the "hairy ones," the shaggy demons of the wilderness; the LXX renders this as: τοῖς ματαίοις (tois mataiois, "to the vain/worthless things, to empty idols"), using μάταιος (mataios, "vain, empty, futile, of no account") — the LXX demythologizes by characterizing the se'irim as mere vanities rather than real demonic entities; EMET preserves "goat-demons" as the literal rendering of se'irim, which in context clearly designates spirit-beings rather than merely goats; some English translations render "goat-demons" (ESV, NJPS), some "satyrs" (KJV), some "demons" (RSV); the choice matters because it reveals whether the text acknowledges the existence of demonic beings or reduces them to mere idolatry; the Hebrew context requires recognition of real spiritual entities being addressed by wilderness sacrifice] after whom [asher hem zonim] [asher hem zonim — "after whom they are going prostituting / committing spiritual fornication"; zonim = qal participle plural of zanah ("to commit fornication, to be a prostitute, to be faithless"); in the Hebrew Bible, unfaithfulness to YHWH — whether through idolatry or worship of other spirits — is consistently expressed with the vocabulary of sexual unfaithfulness (prostitution, adultery); "going after" = following, pursuing; the phrase asher hem zonim achareihem = "after whom they go a-whoring"; LXX: ὀπίσω ὧν αὐτοὶ ἐκπορνεύουσιν ὀπίσω αὐτῶν (opisō hōn autoi ekporneumousin opisō autōn, "after whom they are committing fornication after them"), using ἐκπορνεύω (ekporneuō, "to commit fornication, to be sexually faithless") — the LXX preserves the sexual/spiritual metaphor fully] after them [achareihem] [achareihem — "after them"; the goat-demons; the preposition achar ("after, behind") in the construct of pursuit/following; LXX: ὀπίσω αὐτῶν (opisō autōn, "after them")] — a perpetual statute [chuqqat olam] [chuqqat olam — "a statute of eternity / a perpetual statute"; chuqqah [חֻקָּה] = "statute, prescribed ordinance, something engraved" (from chaqaq, "to engrave, to prescribe"); olam = "eternity, permanent duration, for ever"; the permanent, non-negotiable nature of the prohibition; LXX: νόμιμον αἰώνιον (nomimon aiōnion, "an eternal ordinance"), using νόμιμον (nomimon, "ordinance, statute") and αἰώνιος (aiōnios, "eternal")] shall this be for them [yihyeh zot lahem] [yihyeh zot lahem — "shall this be to them / for them"; the obligation is theirs permanently; LXX: ἔσται αὐτοῖς (estai autois, "shall be to them")] throughout their generations [le-dorotam] [le-dorotam — "for their generations"; the generational scope of the statute; LXX: εἰς τὰς γενεὰς αὐτῶν (eis tas geneas autōn, "for their generations")]:

**8** And to them [va-alehem] [va-alehem — "and to them"; the pronoun refers to all of Israel — the inclusive audience of v.2; LXX: καὶ πρὸς αὐτούς (kai pros autous, "and to them")] you shall say [tomar] [tomar — "you shall say"; the LORD addresses Moshe with his duty to communicate the following; LXX: ἐρεῖς (ereis, "you shall say")]: Any man [ish ish] [ish ish — the doubled distributive as in v.3: "any man whatsoever"; LXX: ἄνθρωπος ἄνθρωπος (anthrōpos anthrōpos, "any man")] from the house of Yisra'el [mi-beit Yisra'el] [mi-beit Yisra'el — "from the house of Yisra'el"; as in v.3; LXX: τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ (tōn huiōn Israēl, "of the sons of Yisra'el")] and from the sojourner [u-min-ha-ger] [u-min-ha-ger — "and from the sojourner"; ha-ger [הַגֵּר] = "the sojourner, the resident alien" (from gur, "to sojourn, to dwell as a stranger"); the ger is a non-Israelite living within the Israelite community — not a slave, not merely passing through, but a resident foreigner who has integrated into the social fabric; the inclusion of the ger in the blood prohibition and the slaughter regulations is significant: the cultic laws bind everyone in the community, not ethnically defined Israelites alone; LXX: τῶν προσηλύτων (tōn prosēlytōn, "of the proselytes"), using προσήλυτος (prosēlytos, "proselyte, resident alien") — the LXX term that will later come to denote a formal convert to Judaism] who sojourns [ha-gar] [ha-gar — "who sojourns / who lives as a stranger among you"; qal participle of gur: "the one who sojourns"; LXX: τῶν προσκειμένων (tōn proskeimenōn, "of those who are attached/joined")] among them [be-tovcham] [be-tovcham — "in their midst / among them"; LXX: ἐν αὐτοῖς (en autois, "among them")] — who offers [asher ya'aleh] [asher ya'aleh — "who offers up / who causes to go up"; hiphil imperfect of 'alah: "to cause to go up, to bring up (a sacrifice)"; the technical term for offering a burnt offering (olah, literally "that which goes up"); LXX: ὃς ἂν ἀνενέγκῃ (hos an anenegkē, "whoever offers up")] a burnt offering [olah] [olah [עֹלָה] — "burnt offering / ascending offering"; from 'alah ("to go up"): the offering that ascends entirely in smoke to the LORD; LXX: ὁλοκαύτωμα (holokautōma, "whole burnt offering"), using ὁλοκαύτωσις / ὁλοκαύτωμα (holokautōma, "whole burnt offering")] or a sacrifice [o-zevach] [o-zevach — "or a sacrifice"; zevach = "slaughtered sacrifice" as in v.5; the category broader than burnt offerings; LXX: ἢ θυσίαν (ē thysian, "or a sacrifice")]:

**9** And he has not brought it [ve-el-petach ohel mo'ed lo' yevi'enu] [ve-el-petach ohel mo'ed lo' yevi'enu — "and to the entrance of the tent of meeting he has not brought it"; the same condition as in v.4 applied now to burnt offerings and sacrifices from any member of the community including sojourners; LXX: καὶ ἐπὶ τὴν θύραν τῆς σκηνῆς τοῦ μαρτυρίου μὴ ἀγάγῃ αὐτό (kai epi tēn thyran tēs skēnēs tou martyriou mē agagē auto, "and to the door of the tent of testimony he does not bring it")] to make it [la-asoto] [la-asoto — "to make/do it / to perform it"; infinitive construct la- + 'asah + suffix -o: "to perform it, to do it (sc. the offering)"; LXX: ὥστε ποιῆσαι αὐτό (hōste poiēsai auto, "so as to perform/make it")] to the LORD [la-YHWH] [la-YHWH — "for the LORD"; directed to YHWH as the recipient; LXX: κυρίῳ (kyriō, "to the LORD")] — that man [ve-nikhrat ha-ish ha-hu'] [ve-nikhrat ha-ish ha-hu' — "and that man shall be cut off"; the same karet verdict as in v.4; LXX: ἐξολεθρευθήσεται ὁ ἄνθρωπος ἐκεῖνος (exolethreuthēsetai ho anthrōpos ekeinos, "that man shall be utterly destroyed")] shall be cut off [ve-nikhrat] from his people [mi-ammo] [mi-ammo — "from his people"; LXX: ἐκ τοῦ λαοῦ αὐτοῦ (ek tou laou autou, "from his people")]:

---

**10** And any man [ve-ish ish] [ve-ish ish — "and a man, a man"; the doubled distributive again; LXX: καὶ ἄνθρωπος ἄνθρωπος (kai anthrōpos anthrōpos, "and any man")] from the house of Yisra'el [mi-beit Yisra'el] [mi-beit Yisra'el — "from the house of Yisra'el"; as in v.3; LXX: τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ (tōn huiōn Israēl, "of the sons of Yisra'el")] and from the sojourners [u-min-ha-gerim] [u-min-ha-gerim — "and from the sojourners / the resident aliens"; plural; as in v.8; LXX: καὶ τῶν προσηλύτων (kai tōn prosēlytōn, "and of the proselytes")] who sojourn [ha-garim] [ha-garim — "who sojourn / who dwell as strangers"; participial plural; LXX: τῶν προσκειμένων (tōn proskeimenōn)] among them [be-tovcham] [be-tovcham — "in their midst"; LXX: ἐν αὐτοῖς (en autois, "among them")] — who eats [asher yokhal] [asher yokhal — "who eats"; qal imperfect of akhal: "to eat"; LXX: ὃς ἂν φάγῃ (hos an phagē, "whoever eats")] any blood [kol-dam] [kol-dam — "any blood / all blood / blood of any kind"; kol = "all, any, every"; dam = "blood"; the absolute totality: no blood whatsoever, of any animal; LXX: πᾶν αἷμα (pan haima, "any blood")] — I will set my face [ve-natatti panai] [ve-natatti panai — "and I will give/set my face"; natan panim = "to set the face against someone" — an expression of divine hostility, opposition, and punitive attention; YHWH himself will oppose the blood-eater; cf. the same expression in 20:3, 5, 6; LXX: καὶ ἐπιστήσω τὸ πρόσωπόν μου (kai epistēsō to prosōpon mou, "and I will set my face"), using ἐπιστήσω (epistēsō, "to set/fix upon"), though many LXX MSS read: στηριῶ τὸ πρόσωπόν μου (stēriō to prosōpon mou)] against [be-nefesh ha-okhelim et-ha-dam] the person [ha-nefesh] [ha-nefesh — "the life/soul/person"; nefesh [נֶפֶשׁ] in this context means the individual person, the living being, the one who eats; the word used here for "person" is nefesh — the same word that will be defined in v.11 as what the blood carries; the irony is exact: the nefesh that eats blood destroys the very nefesh-life that blood represents; LXX: ἐπὶ τὴν ψυχήν (epi tēn psychēn, "against the soul/life"), using ψυχή (psychē, "soul, life, person")] who eats [ha-okhelim] [ha-okhelim — "the one eating / those eating"; participle plural; LXX: τὴν ἐσθίουσαν (tēn esthiousan, "the one eating")] blood [et-ha-dam] [et-ha-dam — "the blood"; LXX: αἷμα (haima, "blood")] — and I will cut him off [ve-hikhratti oto] [ve-hikhratti oto — "and I will cut him off"; hiphil of karat: "I will cause to be cut off"; here YHWH himself is the agent of the karet penalty — not human execution but divine excision from the community; LXX: καὶ ἀπολῶ αὐτόν (kai apolō auton, "and I will destroy him"), using ἀπόλλυμι (apollymi, "to destroy, to ruin utterly")] from the midst of his people [mi-qerev ammo] [mi-qerev ammo — "from the midst of his people"; as in v.4; LXX: ἐκ τοῦ λαοῦ αὐτῶν (ek tou laou autōn, "from their people")]:

**11** For [ki] the life [nefesh] [nefesh [נֶפֶשׁ] — the theological heart of the chapter and one of the most significant statements in the entire Pentateuch: nefesh = "life, soul, living being, vital force, throat (as the seat of breath and appetite), person, self"; the word is extraordinarily multivalent; in this verse it carries its most primal sense: the animating life-force, the vital principle of existence; the nefesh is not the body but what makes the body alive; LXX: ἡ γὰρ ψυχή (hē gar psychē, "for the soul/life"), using ψυχή (psychē, "soul, life, breath")] of the flesh [ha-basar] [ha-basar — "of the flesh / of the body"; basar [בָּשָׂר] = "flesh, body, meat"; here used generically of animal bodies; LXX: τῆς σαρκός (tēs sarkos, "of the flesh"), using σάρξ (sarx, "flesh")] is in the blood [ba-dam hi] [ba-dam hi — "is in the blood / is located in the blood"; ba-dam = "in the blood"; hi = "it is, she is"; the copular pronoun hi ("she/it") completes the predicate: nefesh ha-basar — the life of the flesh — IS (located) in the blood; the statement is a metaphysical-anatomical claim of the highest order in biblical theology: blood is the material vehicle of nefesh; the loss of blood is the loss of life; blood poured out is life poured out; LXX: ἡ ψυχὴ πάσης σαρκὸς ἐν τῷ αἵματί ἐστιν (hē psychē pasēs sarkos en tō haimati estin, "the soul of all flesh is in the blood")] — and I [va-ani] [va-ani — "and I"; the emphatic first-person pronoun; YHWH himself speaks: I am the one who has given it; LXX: κἀγώ (kagō, "and I")] — I have given it [netattiv] [netattiv — "I have given it"; perfect: a completed past action with ongoing effect; YHWH assigned blood to the altar; the blood is not the offerer's to dispose of as he chooses; it was given to him by YHWH for a specific purpose; LXX: δέδωκα αὐτό (dedōka auto, "I have given it"), using the perfect δέδωκα] to you [lakhem] [lakhem — "to you / for you"; the dative plural: "for your benefit, on your behalf"; LXX: ὑμῖν (hymin, "to you")] upon the altar [al-ha-mizbeath] [al-ha-mizbeath — "upon the altar"; the blood is given specifically for altar use; LXX: ἐπὶ τοῦ θυσιαστηρίου (epi tou thysiastēriou, "upon the altar")] to make atonement [le-khapper] [le-khapper — "to make atonement / to effect expiation"; the piel infinitive of kipper: "to cover, to wipe, to make expiation, to atone"; LXX: ἐξιλάσκεσθαι (exilaskesthai, "to make expiation, to propitiate"), using ἐξιλάσκομαι (exilaskomai, "to make propitiation, to placate, to atone")] for your lives [al-nafshoteikem] [al-nafshoteikem — "upon/for your lives / over your persons"; al + nefesh (pl. nafshot) + suffix -kem ("your"): "for your lives, on behalf of your persons"; the blood of the sacrificial animal atones for the nefesh of the offerer; life given for life; blood for blood; LXX: περὶ τῶν ψυχῶν ὑμῶν (peri tōn psychōn hymōn, "concerning your souls/lives")] — for it is the blood [ki ha-dam] [ki ha-dam — "for the blood"; introducing the theological rationale; LXX: τὸ γὰρ αἷμα (to gar haima, "for the blood")] that makes atonement [yekhapper] [yekhapper — "atones / makes atonement"; piel imperfect: "it covers, wipes, makes expiation"; LXX: ἀντὶ τῆς ψυχῆς ἐξιλάσεται (anti tēs psychēs exilasetai, "in exchange for the soul it shall make atonement") — the LXX adds ἀντὶ τῆς ψυχῆς ("in exchange for / as a substitute for the soul"), making the substitutionary logic more explicit than the Hebrew; the Hebrew simply says "the blood atones through/by means of the nefesh"] by means of the life [ba-nefesh] [ba-nefesh — "by means of the life / through the nefesh"; the preposition be- here is instrumental or explicative: the blood atones ba-nefesh, "by virtue of the nefesh it contains" or "through the life that is in it"; the theological argument is complete: blood = nefesh; nefesh = life; therefore blood on the altar = life given for life; LXX: ἐξιλάσεται (exilasetai, "shall make atonement")]:

**12** Therefore [al-ken] [al-ken — "therefore / on account of this"; al-ken introduces the practical consequence of the theological principle; LXX: διὰ τοῦτο (dia touto, "because of this")] I have said [amarti] [amarti — "I have said / I say"; qal perfect: YHWH's previously standing decree; LXX: εἴρηκα (eirēka, "I have said"), using the perfect tense] to the sons of Yisra'el [li-vnei Yisra'el] [li-vnei Yisra'el — "to the sons of Yisra'el"; LXX: τοῖς υἱοῖς Ισραηλ (tois huiois Israēl, "to the sons of Yisra'el")]: 'No person [kol-nefesh mi-kem] among you [kol-nefesh mi-kem] [kol-nefesh mi-kem — "any person from among you / no person of you"; kol-nefesh = "any life/person/soul"; mi-kem = "from among you"; LXX: πᾶσα ψυχὴ ἐξ ὑμῶν (pasa psychē ex hymōn, "every soul from you")] shall eat [lo' yokhal] [lo' yokhal — "shall not eat"; the prohibition repeated; LXX: οὐ φάγεται (ou phagētai, "shall not eat")] blood [dam] — and the sojourner [ve-ha-ger] [ve-ha-ger — "and the sojourner"; as in vv.8, 10; LXX: καὶ ὁ προσήλυτος (kai ho prosēlytos, "and the proselyte")] who sojourns [ha-gar] [ha-gar — "who sojourns"; participial; LXX: ὁ προσκείμενος (ho proskeimenon, "the one joined/attached")] among you [be-tokhekem] [be-tokhekem — "in your midst"; LXX: ἐν ὑμῖν (en hymin, "among you")] shall not eat blood [lo' yokhal dam] [lo' yokhal dam — "shall not eat blood"; the prohibition is absolute, applying equally to native Israelites and resident aliens; LXX: οὐ φάγεται αἷμα (ou phagētai haima, "shall not eat blood")]:

**13** And any man [ve-ish ish] [ve-ish ish — "and a man, a man"; the distributive formula for the fourth time in the chapter; LXX: καὶ ἄνθρωπος ἄνθρωπος (kai anthrōpos anthrōpos)] from the sons of Yisra'el [mi-benei Yisra'el] [mi-benei Yisra'el — "from the sons of Yisra'el"; LXX: τῶν υἱῶν Ισραηλ (tōn huiōn Israēl)] and from the sojourners [u-min-ha-gerim] [u-min-ha-gerim — "and from the sojourners"; LXX: καὶ τῶν προσηλύτων (kai tōn prosēlytōn)] who sojourn [ha-garim] among them [be-tokham] — who hunts [asher yatzud] [asher yatzud — "who hunts"; qal imperfect of tzud: "to hunt"; the activity of hunting brings a case not previously covered by the sacrificial slaughter rules; LXX: ὃς ἂν θηρεύσῃ (hos an thēreusē, "whoever hunts")] game [tzeid] [tzeid [צֵיד] — "game, catch, hunted animal"; shafach = "to pour out, to shed"; in this case not a ritual dashing on an altar but a simple pouring out on the ground; hunted wild animals cannot be brought to the tent of meeting; the blood is dealt with differently — not altar-application but ground-disposal; LXX: καὶ ἐκχεεῖ τὸ αἷμα αὐτοῦ (kai ekcheei to haima autou, "and shall pour out its blood")] and shall cover it [ve-kissahu] [ve-kissahu — "and shall cover it"; piel of kassah: "to cover, to conceal"; LXX: καὶ καλύψει αὐτό (kai kalypsei auto, "and shall cover it")] with dust [be-afar] [be-afar — "with dust / with earth, soil, dirt"; afar [עָפָר] = "dust, loose earth, soil, ashes"; the covering with earth is meaningful: afar is what Adam was formed from (Gen 2:7) and to what he returns (Gen 3:19); the blood-nefesh of the animal returns to the earth as the dust-body of the human does; LXX: τῇ γῇ (tē gē, "with earth"), using γῆ (gē, "earth, soil") — the LXX uses the simpler "earth" rather than "dust"]:

LXX: ψυχή (psychē, "soul/life")] of all flesh [kol-basar] [kol-basar — "of all flesh / of every flesh"; LXX: πάσης σαρκός (pasēs sarkos, "of all flesh")] — its blood [damo] [damo — "its blood / the blood of it"; LXX: τοῖς υἱοῖς Ισραηλ (tois huiois Israēl)]: 'The blood of any flesh [dam kol-basar] [dam kol-basar — "blood of all flesh / blood of any flesh"; the universal scope; LXX: αἷμα πάσης σαρκός (haima pasēs sarkos, "blood of all flesh")] you shall not eat [lo' tokheilu] [lo' tokheilu — "you shall not eat"; ki = causal conjunction; LXX: ὅτι ἡ ψυχή (hoti hē psychē, "because the soul")] of all flesh [kol-basar] is its blood [damo hu'] [damo hu' — "is its blood / the blood of it, it is (= it equals its blood)"; hu' = the copula again; the third statement of the theological principle in slightly varied form; LXX: αὐτοῦ τὸ αἷμά ἐστιν (autou to haima estin, "its blood it is")] — anyone who eats it [kol-okhlav] [kol-okhlav — "all who eat it / anyone who eats it"; **15** And any person [ve-khol-nefesh] [ve-khol-nefesh — "and any person / any life"; nefesh = "person, living being" as in v.12; LXX: καὶ πᾶσα ψυχή (kai pasa psychē, "and every soul/person")] who eats [asher tokhal] [asher tokhal — "who eats"; the transition to a different case: naturally dead or torn animals; LXX: ἥτις φάγεται (hētis phagētai, "whoever eats")] a carcass [nevelah] [nevelah [נְבֵלָה] — "a carcass / that which has died of itself / a naturally dead animal"; nevalah (from naval, "to fall, to die, to wither") = an animal that died on its own — not ritually slaughtered; such an animal has not had its blood properly drained; this is the legal category: animal dead of natural causes or illness; distinct from the treifah (below); LXX: θνησιμαῖον (thnēsimaion, "that which has died of itself, carrion"), using θνησιμαῖος (thnēsimaios, "dead of itself")] or torn [o-treifah] [o-treifah — "or torn (animal)"; as throughout this chapter; LXX: ὁ προσήλυτος (ho prosēlytos, "the proselyte")] — he shall wash his garments [ve-khibes begadav] [ve-khibes begadav — "and shall wash his garments"; kibbes = piel of kabas ("to wash, to trample [cloth], to launder"); the evening marks the end of a ritual day; impurity lasts until sunset; LXX: ἕως ἑσπέρας (heōs hesperas, "until evening"), using ἑσπέρα (hespera, "evening")] — and he shall be clean [ve-taher] [ve-taher — "and he shall be clean / pure"; qal perfect: upon sunset, the cleansing is complete; LXX: καὶ καθαρὸς ἔσται (kai katharos estai, "and shall be clean/pure"), using καθαρός (katharos, "pure, clean")]:

**16** But if he does not wash [ve-im lo' yekhabbes] [ve-im lo' yekhabbes — "and if he does not wash"; the conditional: the consequence if the required washing is neglected; yekhabbes = piel imperfect; LXX: ἐὰν δὲ μὴ πλύνῃ (ean de mē plynē, "but if he does not wash")] and his flesh is not bathed [u-vesaro lo' yirchatz] [u-vesaro lo' yirchatz — "and his flesh is not bathed / and he does not bathe his body"; besaro = "his flesh/body"; the second neglect: both garments and body must be washed; LXX: καὶ τὸ σῶμα μὴ λούσηται (kai to sōma mē lousētai, "and the body is not washed")] — then he shall bear his iniquity [ve-nasa' avono] [ve-nasa' avono — "and shall bear his iniquity / he shall carry his guilt"; nasa' avon = "to lift up iniquity, to carry guilt"; the legal formula for personal accountability for one's own transgression; the failure to wash after contact with a carcass is not merely an oversight — it is a culpable offense; LXX: καὶ λήμψεται τὴν ἀδικίαν αὐτοῦ (kai lēmpsetai tēn adikian autou, "and shall receive/take up his iniquity/injustice"), using ἀδικία (adikia, "injustice, wrongdoing, unrighteousness")]:

## Translator's Notes — Key Terms and Exegetical Issues

### 1. Dam yechashev lo — "Blood shall be imputed to him" (v.4)

The phrase dam yechashev la-ish ha-hu' ("blood shall be imputed to that man") is juridically and theologically extraordinary. The verb chashav [חָשַׁב] belongs to the semantic domain of reckoning, calculating, and imputing. In legal contexts it means: an act is "counted against" a person's account. Here, the unauthorized slaughter of an animal at a location other than the tabernacle entrance is formally "imputed" as bloodguilt — the equivalent of shedding human blood. The theological logic is: an animal has nefesh; its blood contains its nefesh; unauthorized shedding of that nefesh-bearing blood is treated as equivalent to murder. The LXX renders this with logizomai ("to reckon, to impute"), the same verb Paul employs in Romans 4 and Galatians 3 for the imputation of righteousness. The translational range of chashav/logizomai — from judicial reckoning to theological imputation — makes this one of the most pregnant verb choices in the Hebrew Bible.

### 2. La-se'irim — "To the goat-demons" (v.7)

Se'irim [שְׂעִירִים] (singular sa'ir [שָׂעִיר]) means literally "hairy ones" and is applied in three distinct contexts: (a) a male goat or he-goat (the standard sense in Lev 16's "lot for Azazel" passages); (b) a demon, spirit, or supernatural being (the sense here and in Isa 13:21, 34:14); (c) a satyr in Greek mythological equivalence. In v.7 the context is explicitly cultic: Yisra'el has been "going a-whoring after" these beings (zonim achareihem). The prohibition is against sacrificing to them. The se'irim are spirit-beings inhabiting desert places, conceptually related to the demonic beings to whom the Azazel goat was sent in the wilderness (Lev 16:10). Whether these beings "really existed" or were merely believed to exist, the text treats them as addressable cultic entities requiring a prohibition. The LXX's rendering as ta mataia ("the vain/empty things, the vanities") is a polemical translation that refuses to acknowledge the beings' existence, reducing them to "nothings" (cf. the LXX's regular treatment of foreign gods as mataia, "vanities"). EMET renders them "goat-demons" following the philological analysis and the contextual requirement of the passage.

### 3. Ki nefesh ha-basar ba-dam hi — "For the life of the flesh is in the blood" (v.11)

This is the theological center of Leviticus 17 and arguably one of the most significant theological statements in the entire Pentateuch. Three interrelated claims are made:

(a) Ontological: the nefesh of flesh — its animating life-force, the vital principle that makes a body alive rather than dead — is located in or identified with the blood. Blood is not merely a liquid; it is the physical bearer of life itself.

(b) Cultic: YHWH has given (natatti) this blood to Israel. The blood is not Israel's to consume; it was given to them by the LORD for a specific purpose. The proper use of blood is altar-application.

(c) Atonement-logic: the blood on the altar makes atonement ba-nefesh ("through/by the nefesh") for the nefesh of the worshipper. The transaction is nefesh for nefesh: the life of the animal given on the altar substitutes for and covers the life of the human offerer. This is the foundational atonement logic of the entire sacrificial system.

The statement is repeated in structural variations in vv.14 (bi-nafsho hu' = "its blood is its life") confirming its centrality. The LXX in v.11 expands: "I have given it to you upon the altar to make atonement for your souls, for the blood shall make atonement ἀντὶ τῆς ψυχῆς (anti tēs psychēs, 'in exchange for / as a substitute for the soul')" — the LXX makes the substitutionary logic explicit with anti ("in exchange for, instead of").

### 4. Nefesh — Translation Range

| Hebrew | EMET Rendering | Notes |
|--------|---------------|-------|
| nefesh ha-basar (v.11) | "the life of the flesh" | Vital animating principle |
| be-nafsho hu' (v.14) | "is its life" | Blood = life |
| kol-nefesh (v.15) | "any person" | Individual human being |
| nasa' avono (v.16) | "bear his iniquity" | Personal guilt-accountability |

### 5. Nevalah and Treifah (v.15)

These two categories create the foundational taxonomy of unfit animals that will govern Rabbinic kashrut:
- **Nevalah** [נְבֵלָה]: dead of itself — no ritual slaughter performed; blood not properly drained.
- **Treifah** [טְרֵפָה]: torn by a wild animal — physically damaged; blood contaminated by violent death.

Both categories involve blood that has not been properly managed through authorized slaughter. The consumption of such animals is not a karet offense (as blood-eating is) but generates impurity requiring laundering of garments and bodily immersion. The difference in penalty (karet vs. impurity) reflects the distinction between the theological principle (blood prohibition) and the subsidiary ritual concern (contamination by improper animal death).

### 6. Structural Architecture

The chapter is built around three deployments of the ish ish distributive ("any man, a man, a man") in vv.3, 8, 10, 13 — and three iterations of the blood prohibition in vv.10, 12, 14. The triple repetition of the blood prohibition (a literary device of intensification) mirrors the triple use of the karet penalty (vv.4, 9, 14). No other chapter in Leviticus uses the karet sentence three times for the same offense. The rhetorical effect is to establish the blood prohibition as the most serious cultic regulation in the chapter — more serious even than unauthorized slaughter (vv.3–9), which receives only two karet sentences (vv.4, 9).

| Hebrew | Transliteration | EMET Rendering | Notes |

| נֶפֶשׁ | nefesh | "life / person / soul" | Context-determined; the chapter's keyword |
| דָּם | dam | "blood" | Physical blood as bearer of nefesh |
| כִּפֶּר | kipper | "make atonement / atone" | Piel; covers, wipes, expiates |
| חָשַׁב | chashav | "impute / reckon / account" | Niphal passive in v.4 |
| כָּרַת | karat | "cut off" | karet penalty; divine or communal excision |
| שְׂעִירִים | se'irim | "goat-demons / hairy ones" | Demonic beings; LXX: "vain things" |
| זֹנִים | zonim | "going a-whoring / committing fornication" | Spiritual unfaithfulness expressed sexually |
| גֵּר | ger | "sojourner / resident alien" | Non-Israelite dwelling in the community |
| אֶזְרָח | ezrach | "native" | Home-born Israelite; contrasted with ger |
| נְבֵלָה | nevalah | "carcass / naturally dead animal" | Animal that died without ritual slaughter |
| טְרֵפָה | treifah | "torn animal" | Animal mauled by a wild beast |
| זֶבַח | zevach | "sacrifice" | Slaughtered offering, any type |
| שְׁלָמִים | shelamim | "peace offerings" | From shalom; communal shared sacrifice |
| עֹלָה | olah | "burnt offering" | "Ascending offering"; wholly consumed |
| קָרְבָּן | qorban | "offering / gift" | Generic term: that which is brought near |
| חֵלֶב | chelev | "fat" | Suet/internal fat; reserved for YHWH |
| רֵיחַ נִיחֹחַ | reyach nichoach | "soothing aroma" | Divine pleasure at accepted sacrifice |
| עָפָר | afar | "dust / earth" | Used to cover poured-out blood (v.13) |
| חֻקַּת עוֹלָם | chuqqat olam | "perpetual statute" | The permanent, engraved, eternal ordinance |
